effectText: You can't see and automatically fail any ability check that requires sight.
adjudicationNote: The ability-check auto-failure depends on whether a check requires sight; the mask records the sensory affordance only.

effectText: Attack rolls against you have Advantage, and your attack rolls have Disadvantage.
adjudicationNote: Attack-roll advantage and disadvantage are roll modifiers, not action masks.

effectText: You can't attack the charmer or target the charmer with damaging abilities or magical effects.
adjudicationNote: The mask is source-scoped to the charmer and does not prohibit harm against others.

effectText: The charmer has Advantage on any ability check to interact with you socially.
adjudicationNote: Social interaction advantage is a roll modifier, not an action mask.

effectText: You can't hear and automatically fail any ability check that requires hearing.
adjudicationNote: The ability-check auto-failure depends on whether a check requires hearing; the mask records the sensory affordance only.

effectText: This condition is cumulative. Each time you receive it, you gain 1 Exhaustion level. You die if your Exhaustion level is 6.
adjudicationNote: Exhaustion levels are cumulative condition state, not an affordance mask.

effectText: When you make a D20 Test, the roll is reduced by 2 times your Exhaustion level.
adjudicationNote: The roll reduction is mechanical but is not an action mask.

effectText: Your Speed is reduced by a number of feet equal to 5 times your Exhaustion level.
adjudicationNote: Speed reduction may limit movement distance without masking Move entirely.

effectText: Finishing a Long Rest removes 1 of your Exhaustion levels. When your Exhaustion level reaches 0, the condition ends.
adjudicationNote: Recovery timing is condition lifecycle data, not an action mask.

effectText: You have Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls while the source of fear is within line of sight.
adjudicationNote: The line-of-sight roll modifier is not an action mask.

effectText: You can't willingly move closer to the source of fear.
adjudicationNote: The mask applies only to willing movement closer to the fear source.

effectText: Your Speed is 0 and can't increase.
You have Disadvantage on attack rolls against any target other than the grappler.
adjudicationNote: The attack-roll disadvantage is a roll modifier, not an action mask.

effectText: The grappler can drag or carry you when it moves, but every foot of movement costs it 1 extra foot unless you are Tiny or two or more sizes smaller than it.
adjudicationNote: Dragging or carrying the grappled target depends on grappler movement and relative size.

effectText: You can't take any action, Bonus Action, or Reaction.
Your Concentration is broken.
You can't speak.
If you're Incapacitated when you roll Initiative, you have Disadvantage on the roll.
adjudicationNote: Initiative disadvantage is a roll modifier, not an action mask.

effectText: If you're Invisible when you roll Initiative, you have Advantage on the roll.
adjudicationNote: Initiative advantage is a roll modifier, not an action mask.

effectText: You aren't affected by any effect that requires its target to be seen unless the effect's creator can somehow see you. Any equipment you are wearing or carrying is also concealed.
adjudicationNote: Sight-required targeting is source/observer dependent; this package records the prose rather than inventing a targeting engine.

effectText: Attack rolls against you have Disadvantage, and your attack rolls have Advantage. If a creature can somehow see you, you don't gain this benefit against that creature.
adjudicationNote: Attack-roll advantage and disadvantage are roll modifiers, not action masks.

effectText: You have the Incapacitated condition.
adjudicationNote: The action masks derive from the embedded Incapacitated condition.

effectText: Your Speed is 0 and can't increase.
You automatically fail Strength and Dexterity saving throws.
adjudicationNote: Automatic save failure is mechanical but is not an action mask.

effectText: Attack rolls against you have Advantage.
adjudicationNote: Attack-roll advantage is a roll modifier, not an action mask.

effectText: Any attack roll that hits you is a Critical Hit if the attacker is within 5 feet of you.
adjudicationNote: Critical-hit conversion is attack resolution data, not an action mask.

effectText: You are transformed, along with any nonmagical objects you are wearing and carrying, into a solid inanimate substance (usually stone). Your weight increases by a factor of ten, and you cease aging.
adjudicationNote: The transformation affects physical state and carried nonmagical objects; no action mask is invented here.

effectText: You have the Incapacitated condition.
adjudicationNote: The action masks derive from the embedded Incapacitated condition.

effectText: Your Speed is 0 and can't increase.
Attack rolls against you have Advantage.
adjudicationNote: Attack-roll advantage is a roll modifier, not an action mask.

effectText: You automatically fail Strength and Dexterity saving throws.
adjudicationNote: Automatic save failure is mechanical but is not an action mask.

effectText: You have Resistance to all damage.
adjudicationNote: Damage resistance is not an action mask.

effectText: You have Immunity to the Poisoned condition.
adjudicationNote: Condition immunity is not an action mask.

effectText: You have Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.
adjudicationNote: Attack-roll and ability-check disadvantage are roll modifiers, not action masks.

effectText: Your only movement options are to crawl or to spend an amount of movement equal to half your Speed (round down) to right yourself and thereby end the condition. If your Speed is 0, you can't right yourself.
adjudicationNote: Prone constrains movement modes and standing cost but does not mask Move entirely.

effectText: You have Disadvantage on attack rolls. An attack roll against you has Advantage if the attacker is within 5 feet of you. Otherwise, that attack roll has Disadvantage.
adjudicationNote: Attack-roll advantage and disadvantage are roll modifiers, not action masks.

effectText: Your Speed is 0 and can't increase.
Attack rolls against you have Advantage, and your attack rolls have Disadvantage.
adjudicationNote: Attack-roll advantage and disadvantage are roll modifiers, not action masks.

effectText: You have Disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws.
adjudicationNote: Dexterity-save disadvantage is a roll modifier, not an action mask.

adjudicationNote: Automatic save failure is mechanical but is not an action mask.

effectText: Attack rolls against you have Advantage.
adjudicationNote: Attack-roll advantage is a roll modifier, not an action mask.

effectText: You have the Incapacitated and Prone conditions, and you drop whatever you're holding. When this condition ends, you remain Prone.
adjudicationNote: The action masks derive from the embedded Incapacitated condition; dropping held objects and remaining Prone are recorded as prose.

effectText: Your Speed is 0 and can't increase.
Attack rolls against you have Advantage.

effectText: You're unaware of your surroundings.
adjudicationNote: Awareness is observer/context dependent; this package keeps the source prose without inventing a perception engine.
